# Matthew Chapter 24 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_024_page_001.jpg, MAT_024_page_002.jpg

### The Destruction of the Temple Predicted

1. As Jesus was leaving the temple, his disciples came up to point out the temple buildings to him.

2. "Do you see all of this?" he replied. "I tell you the truth, not one stone here will be left standing on another. Everything will be demolished."

3. Later, Jesus sat on the Mount of Olives. His disciples came to him privately and asked, "Tell us — when will all this happen? And what will be the sign of your coming and the end of the age?"

### Signs Before the End

4. Jesus answered, "Be careful that no one deceives you.

5. Many will come claiming to be me, saying, 'I am the Messiah,' and they will fool many people.

6. You'll hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't panic. These things must happen, but the end hasn't come yet.

7. Nations will go to war against each other, kingdoms against kingdoms. There will be famines and earthquakes in many parts of the world.

8. But all this is only the beginning — like the first contractions before childbirth.

9. Then you will be arrested, persecuted, and killed. Every nation will hate you because of me.

10. Many will turn away from the faith, betraying and hating each other.

11. Many false prophets will appear and deceive many people.

12. Because of the surge of evil, most people's love will grow cold.

13. But the one who endures to the end will be saved.

14. And this good news of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the entire world as a witness to every nation. Then the end will come.

### The Great Tribulation

15. So when you see the 'devastating desecration' that the prophet Daniel spoke about standing in the holy place — reader, pay attention! —

16. then those in Judea must escape to the mountains.

17. If you're on the roof, don't go back inside for your belongings.

18. If you're out in the field, don't go back for your coat.

19. How dreadful it will be for pregnant women and nursing mothers in those days!

20. Pray that your escape won't be in winter or on a Sabbath,

21. because there will be suffering worse than anything the world has ever seen or ever will see.

22. Unless those days are cut short, no one will survive. But for the sake of God's chosen people, those days will be shortened.

23. If anyone tells you then, 'Look, here's the Messiah!' or 'He's over there!' — don't believe it.

24. False messiahs and false prophets will appear and produce spectacular signs and wonders to deceive, if possible, even God's chosen people.

25. I'm warning you ahead of time.

26. So if anyone says, 'He's out in the desert!' — don't go. 'He's hiding in a secret room!' — don't believe it.

27. For the coming of the Son of Man will be as unmistakable as lightning that flashes across the entire sky from east to west.

28. Where the carcass is, the vultures will gather.

### The Coming of the Son of Man

29. Immediately after the anguish of those days, the sun will be darkened, the moon will give no light, the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers of heaven will be shaken.

30. Then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in the sky, and all the peoples of the earth will mourn. They will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and breathtaking glory.

31. He will send out his angels with a mighty blast of a trumpet, and they will gather his chosen people from the four corners of the earth — from one end of the sky to the other.

### The Lesson of the Fig Tree

32. Learn a lesson from the fig tree: when its branches bud and its leaves begin to sprout, you know summer is near.

33. In the same way, when you see all these things happening, you'll know that the time is near — right at the door.

34. I tell you the truth, this generation will not pass away before all these things take place.

35. Heaven and earth will disappear, but my words will never disappear.

### No One Knows the Day or Hour

36. But no one knows the day or hour when these things will happen — not the angels in heaven, not even the Son. Only the Father knows.

37. When the Son of Man returns, it will be like the days of Noah.

38. In the days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, getting married and giving their children in marriage — right up until Noah entered the ark.

39. They didn't realize what was happening until the flood swept them all away. That's how it will be when the Son of Man comes.

40. Two men will be working in a field; one will be taken, the other left.

41. Two women will be grinding grain at the mill; one will be taken, the other left.

42. So stay alert! You don't know what day your Lord is coming.

43. Understand this: if the homeowner had known what time of night the burglar was coming, he would have stayed awake and not let his house be broken into.

44. You must be ready too, because the Son of Man will come when you least expect him.

### The Faithful and Unfaithful Servants

45. A faithful, sensible servant is one the master can trust to manage the household and give the other servants their food at the right time.

46. How fortunate that servant is when the master returns and finds him doing his job!

47. I tell you the truth, the master will put that servant in charge of everything he owns.

48. But what if the servant is wicked and thinks, 'My master won't be back for a while,'

49. and starts beating the other servants, partying, and getting drunk?

50. The master will return on a day the servant doesn't expect and at a time he doesn't know.

51. He will cut that servant to pieces and assign him a place with the hypocrites, where there will be weeping and grinding of teeth.

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 3 — The disciples' question:** They ask about three things that they assume are one: the temple's destruction, Jesus' coming, and the end of the age. Jesus' answer weaves together near and far horizons. The destruction of Jerusalem (70 CE) and the final return of the Son of Man overlap in prophetic vision — a phenomenon scholars call "prophetic telescoping."

**Verse 12 — "love will grow cold":** The Greek ψυγήσεται (from ψύχω, "to cool") is the opposite of the burning love described in the greatest commandment. Lawlessness doesn't just produce evil deeds — it refrigerates love itself. This is perhaps the most chilling prediction in the discourse.

**Verse 28 — "where the carcass is, vultures gather":** A proverb meaning: the signs of the end will be unmistakable, just as vultures circling make a carcass obvious. Don't go looking for secret signs — when it happens, everyone will know.

**Verse 36 — "not even the Son":** The most radical verse in the chapter. Jesus admits he doesn't know when the end comes. This is not a failure of divinity but an expression of genuine incarnation — the Son fully entering human limitation. If Jesus doesn't know, no human teacher should claim to.

**Verses 40-41 — "one taken, one left":** Often read as describing a "rapture," but in context (the Noah comparison), being "taken" parallels being swept away by the flood — judgment, not rescue. Being "left" means surviving. The point is not escape but readiness.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
